content:
Member-only story

I thought I was building another productivity tool. Instead, I ended up creating an AI system that researched competitors, summarized meetings, wrote proposals, generated reports, and even suggested new business ideas while I focused on work that actually required my attention. Looking back, it wasn’t the AI itself that changed my business — it was the workflow around it.

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Google DeepMind on Unsplash1. I Didn’t Need More AI Tools — I Needed One System That Connected Everything
When AI tools started appearing everywhere, I did what most developers did.
I signed up for all of them.
One tool for writing.
Another for research.
A third for meeting notes.
Then one for coding.
Another for image generation.
A browser extension.
A desktop app.
